item.title: Flash flood in Moulvibazar, Netrakona
item.description: People at about 15 villages under Sadar and Joychandi unions at Kulaura upazila in Moulvibazar have been marooned, and crops and fisheries submerged after the embankment of Gogalichhara, a local canal, collapsed on Tuesday night due to heavy rainfall and an onrush of water from the upstream region across the border......

item.title: Downpour leaves Ctg roads under water for second day
item.description: Incessant rain on Wednesday again sent different areas of Chattogram city under water for the second day running, seriously disrupting normal life and causing immense suffering to city dwellers......

item.title: Wet spell to continue for a week
item.description: The current inclement weather will continue for about a week more, bringing thunderstorms and heavy rain in most parts of the country until May 4–5, said officials at the Bangladesh Meteorological Department on Wednesday......

item.title: Ministries, divs asked not to make extra spending
item.description: The Finance Division on Wednesday asked the ministries and the other divisions not to make extra expenditures violating rules......

item.title: Row erupts in JS over claims on 1971, July uprising
item.description: Jatiya Sangsad witnessed a tense and politically charged debate on Wednesday as treasury bench members alleged that Jamaat-e-Islami bore historical responsibility for opposing the birth of Bangladesh during the 1971 War of Liberation and that the opposition was attempting to turn the July uprising into apoliticalbusiness......Bangladesh travel guide

item.title: Measles vaccination gaps widen since 2020
item.description: Routine measles vaccination coverage in Bangladesh has declined since 2020, widening immunity gaps that experts say have played a critical role in the ongoing measles outbreak......Breakingnews alerts

item.title: Govt proposes JS body for constitution amendment
item.description: Law, justice and parliamentary affairs minister Md Asaduzzaman on Wednesday proposed the formation of a 17-member special parliamentary committee to amend the constitution in line with the July National Charter......

item.title: Linear vs orthogonal journalism
item.description: MOST journalism moves in a straight line. It tells us what happened, then what followed and then what came next. This linear structure is effective for reporting events, but it falls short when the task is to explain reality. Complex systems do not operate along a single line — the dynamics of a single line, if you will. They evolve through the simultaneous interaction......

item.title: Why moves underdeliver
item.description: BANGLADESH has invested significantly in entrepreneurship through public policy. Startup Bangladesh, iDEA, the Digital Entrepreneurship and Innovation Ecosystem Development project, the Hi-Tech Park Authority’s accelerator programme — the list of government-backed initiatives is not short. The stated ambitions are real. The institutional machinery is in......Bangladeshtravel guide

item.title: Surveillance, AI and not forgetting ourselves
item.description: THERE is a long and unbroken thread running through human history, though it is often described as a series of revolutions. Stone to paper. Paper to print. Print to computers. Computers to the internet. The internet to apps. Apps to artificial intelligence. It is usually told as a story of acceleration. But it is more accurately a story of extension......

item.title: Precision medicine and future of health care
item.description: WHEN a DNA test can reveal not only ancestry but also how a person’s body responds to specific medications, healthcare begins to shift from generalisation to individualisation. This is the promise of pharmacogenetic testing, an emerging field within precision medicine that studies how genetic differences influence drug response. It marks a departure from traditional......

item.title: School education paradox
item.description: MANY private schools today look like corporate headquarters rather than places of learning. Tall gates, air-conditioned corridors, smart boards, glossy brochures, international affiliations and a fee structure that signals prestige. Parents often interpret such visible signals as the proof of quality. In many cases, private schools do deliver good outcomes with structured......

item.title: Rising police suicide reflects institutional neglect
item.description: A RISE in suicide among low-ranking police personnel is a troubling indicator of institutional neglect and can no longer be dismissed as isolated incidents. Official data show that 124 personnel committed suicide in 2010–April 2026, including 20 since January 2025. Studies and agency insiders say that chronic overwork, poor living conditions and acute financial distress......

item.title: Grim murder, rape figures call for action
item.description: THE minister for home affairs said in the parliament on April 28 that 464 cases of murder and 666 cases of rape had been filed across the country since February 17, when the Bangladesh Nationalist Party assumed office. It might well be the case that many of the cases filed after February 17 may have been in connection with murders and rapes that happened earlier. The......

item.title: Arsenal, Atletico trade penalties in Champions League semi-final draw
item.description:
Julian Alvarez's penalty secured Atletico Madrid a 1-1 draw against Arsenal in a nervy Champions League semi-final first leg clash on Wednesday.
...International news coverage

item.title: Govt highlights CHT accord progress at UN
item.description: Bangladesh has highlighted progress in implementing the Chittagong Hill Tracts Peace Accord and measures for ethnic communities’ development at the United Nations......International news coverage

item.title: UNESCO to support police training in Bangladesh
item.description: A delegation from UNESCO met with home minister Salahuddin Ahmed at his office in the Jatiya Sangsad on Wednesday, where both sides discussed cooperation in police training, media relations, and freedom of expression......

item.title: Ex-minister Mosharraf’s 105 accounts frozen
item.description: A Dhaka court on Wednesday ordered the freezing of 105 accounts belonging to former expatriates’ welfare and overseas employment minister Khandaker Mosharraf Hossain over graft allegations......

item.title: Schoolgirl dies after headscarf caught in auto-van wheel
item.description: A 12-year-old schoolgirl died after her headscarf became entangled in the wheel of a moving auto-van in Pirgachha upazila of Rangpur Wednesday morning......

item.title: Public awareness, enforcement of laws stressed to curb noise pollution
item.description: Speakers at a discussion on Wednesday stressed public awareness and proper enforcement of laws to curb noise pollution......Bangladesh travel guide

item.title: 17 citizens protest against issuing warning letter to Chakma Rani Yan Yan
item.description: Citizens, including rights activists and teachers, on Wednesday expressed protest and condemnation over a warning letter the Rangamati district administration issued to human rights activist and Chakma Circle Rani Yan Yan, alleging her involvement in ‘false propaganda’ against the state and the army......

item.title: Ex-DGFI director Naser remanded for 3rd time
item.description: A Dhaka court on Wednesday placed former DGFI director Afzal Naser on a fresh three-day remand in a case filed over the killing of Mahmudul Hasan in the Uttara area during July uprising......

item.title: Israel must fully implement truce as strikes continue: Lebanon
item.description: Lebanese president Joseph Aoun said Wednesday that Israel must fully implement the ceasefire between the two countries before beginning direct talks, after Israeli strikes killed more than 20 people over the last two days......

item.title: US SC curbs race-based voting maps
item.description: The US Supreme Court on Wednesday sharply limited the use of race in drawing electoral districts ahead of midterm elections......Geographic Reference

item.title: Final phase of West Bengal elections held
item.description: Around 91.41 per cent of the 3.21 crore electors voted till 7:00pm of the second phase of polling in West Bengal amid sporadic violence, reports The Hindu......

item.title: One more held over lynching of Kushtia pir
item.description: The police arrested a tenth-grader in connection with the lynching of pir Abdul Rahman at Kushtia’s Daulatpur upazila......

item.title: BGB seizes 10,000 Yaba pills in Cumilla
item.description: Border Guard Bangladesh personnel seized 10,000 pieces of Yaba tablets, worth an estimated Tk 30.60 crore from a border area in Cumilla during an early morning drive on Wednesday......

item.title: UIU holds training on administrative excellence
item.description: The Institutional Quality Assurance Cell of United International University organised a developing professional skills training titled Administrative Excellence: Induction and Capability Development Programme at the UIU in Dhaka recently......

item.title: Indian assistant HC calls on RUET VC
item.description: Indian assistant high commissioner to Bangladesh Manoj Kumar called on vice-chancellor of the Rajshahi University of Engineering and Technology Professor SM Abdur Razzak at the VC office in Rajshahi on Tuesday......

item.title: College student dies after consuming alcohol in Rajshahi
item.description: A college student died after falling ill allegedly from consuming alcohol in Rajshahi city......

item.title: Tutor sentenced to death for schoolgirl murder in Noakhali
item.description: A Dhaka court on Wednesday sentenced a private tutor to death for the brutal murder of 14-year-old schoolgirl Tasnia Hossain Adita in Noakhali municipality in 2022......

item.title: IP protection vital for safeguarding innovation: GAU VC
item.description: Gazipur Agricultural University vice-chancellor Professor GKM Mustafizur Rahman at a seminar at the GAU on Wednesday said that fostering a culture of intellectual property protection was essential for taking university-led research and innovation to global standards......

item.title: DU holds orientation for new students
item.description: The University of Dhaka held an orientation for new students of first year undergraduate programme at the auditorium of Teachers-Students Centre of the university on Wednesday......

item.title: South Asian agriculture Regional meet stresses stronger collaboration
item.description: Speakers at a regional meeting in Dhaka city have called for strengthening regional cooperation and building robust public-private partnerships to ensure food security and sustainable agricultural development across South Asia......

item.title: Dhaka seeks future-focused EU partnership
item.description: Bangladesh on Wednesday underscored its interest in advancing a forward-looking partnership with the European Union, infusing fresh dynamism into bilateral engagement to realise the untapped potential of the relations......Bangladesh travel guide

item.title: NSU hosts awareness session on immunisation for all
item.description: North South University organised an awareness session titled Vaccination for All, marking World Immunisation Week 2026, on its campus in Dhaka on Tuesday......

item.title: Trump to put his picture in US passports
item.description: An image of Donald Trump will soon appear in some US passports, officials said Tuesday, shattering another norm as the president aggressively puts his personal stamp on government institutions......

item.title: 47pc of compensation imposed on polluters realised
item.description: Environment, forest and climate change affairs minister Abdul Awal Mintoo on Wednesday told Jatiya Sangsad that the authorities had imposed Tk 622.80 crore in compensation on 18,617 polluting establishments across the country between July 13, 2010 and March 31, 2026......

item.title: Family files case against unnamed people, none arrested, identified
item.description: A murder case was filed against unnamed people with the New Market police station on Wednesday over the killing of listed crime suspect Khandoker Naim Ahmed Titon, 42, in the capital’s New Market area on the previous day......Geographic Reference

item.title: 24 women raped at Savar, Ashulia in four months
item.description: At least 24 rape cases were reported in the industrial belt of Savar and Ashulia in the outskirts of Dhaka city in the first four months of the year, highlighting growing safety concerns for women and children, police officials said......

item.title: Pry students to get dress, bag from July, says minister
item.description: Education minister ANM Ehsanul Hoque Milon on Wednesday said as per the government’s election promise, the piloting programme of the distribution of free school dresses, shoes and jute bags among government primary school students will begin in next July......
